text.title: architecture-decision-records Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for architecture-decision-records as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent writes ADR with only the chosen option and no alternatives section, or with alternatives that are clearly strawmen.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs default to validating the chosen option rather than steelmanning alternatives. Require the agent to explicitly argue for each alternative before explaining rejection. If an alternative has no strong case, it was not seriously considered.

failure-mode.description: Agent lists only positive consequences or downplays negative ones to make the decision look better.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are trained to be helpful and tend to justify chosen options. Negative consequences are not failures — they are honest tradeoffs. Require the agent to list at least one negative consequence for any non-trivial decision.
